content: Treatment approach based on BMI and comorbidities. Lifestyle intervention foundation: caloric deficit (500-750 kcal/day deficit produces 0.5-0.75 kg/week loss), reduced-calorie diet (Mediterranean, DASH, low-carbohydrate all effective), physical activity ≥150 min/week, behavioral strategies. Pharmacotherapy indicated for BMI ≥30 or ≥27 with comorbidities: GLP-1 receptor agonists (semaglutide 2.4mg weekly - STEP trials showed ~15% weight loss; liraglutide 3mg daily); dual GIP/GLP-1 agonist tirzepatide (~20% weight loss); phentermine/topiramate ER; naltrexone/bupropion; orlistat. Metabolic/bariatric surgery for BMI ≥40 or ≥35 with comorbidities: Roux-en-Y gastric bypass (~25-30% total weight loss), sleeve gastrectomy (~20-25%), biliopancreatic diversion (most effective but highest risk). Surgery improves/remits T2DM, reduces mortality. Long-term follow-up essential for nutritional deficiencies.
